name: CustomTkinter (Modern-Looking Tkinter UI Framework)

performance.notes: Tkinter backend; lightweight; no OpenGL required; good for simple UIs

agent_readiness.notes: Tkinter with modern styling and dark mode. import customtkinter as ctk. app = ctk.CTk(). app.geometry('400x300'). btn = ctk.CTkButton(app, text='Click Me', command=callback). btn.pack(). entry = ctk.CTkEntry(app). slider = ctk.CTkSlider(app). checkbox = ctk.CTkCheckBox(app). combobox = ctk.CTkComboBox(app). Dark/light mode: ctk.set_appearance_mode('dark'). Themes: blue, dark-blue, green. No extra dependencies beyond Pillow. Pre-built widgets: scrollable frame, tabview, segmented button.

gotchas: Drop-in Tkinter replacement only for widgets — some Tkinter internals differ
Less widget variety than Qt (no table view, limited charting)
Not for complex apps — performance degrades above ~100 widgets
Theme customization beyond built-in themes requires CSS-like knowledge
best_when: Simple desktop utilities with modern UI without Qt complexity
Quick internal tools for Python scripts
When Tkinter's default ugly style is the only barrier
avoid_when: When built-in Tkinter is already sufficient
summary: CustomTkinter modernizes Tkinter with dark mode and clean styling. ctk.CTk() app with CTkButton, CTkEntry, CTkSlider, CTkComboBox widgets. set_appearance_mode('dark'). Three themes: blue, dark-blue, green. No extra dependencies beyond Pillow. Drop-in upgrade over Tkinter for quick desktop utilities. Not for complex apps — use PyQt6/PySide6 for full-featured GUIs. MIT license.
